'''Scott William Donald''' (born 14 February 1980) is an Australian former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played in the 1990s and 2000s. He played in the [[National Rugby League]] for Australian clubs, [[North Queensland Cowboys]], [[Parramatta Eels]] and [[Manly-Warringah Sea Eagles]], and in the [[Super League]] for English club, [[Leeds Rhinos]], usually on the {{rlp|wg}}.

==Playing career==
===National Rugby League===
Scott Donald was born in [[Townsville]], [[Queensland]], and made his [[National Rugby League]] (NRL) début for the [[North Queensland Cowboys]] in Round 19 of the [[1998 NRL season]]: a 14–10 win over the [[Adelaide Rams]] at the [[Willows Sports Complex|Malanda Stadium]] in [[Townsville]]. Donald only played four games in three seasons with the Cowboys before signing with [[Sydney]] based team the [[Parramatta Eels]] at the end of the [[2000 NRL season|2000 season]].

Donald made 14 appearances for Parramatta in [[2001 NRL season|2001]], but injury saw him miss the [[2001 NRL Grand Final]] loss to the [[Newcastle Knights]]. Injury restricted him to just seven games in [[2002 NRL season|2002]], and at the end of the year he left the Parramatta club to join the [[Manly-Warringah Sea Eagles]].

At Manly, Donald would finally cement a first grade spot on the wing and he played in 68 games for the Manly side over three seasons and was the club's leading try scorer in [[2003 NRL season|2003]], crossing for 21 tries in 24 games.

===Super League===
After three seasons at Manly in which he scored 48 tries in 68 games, Donald left the NRL and joined English [[Super League]] club the [[Leeds Rhinos]] at the back end of 2005 as a replacement for outgoing winger [[Mark Calderwood]]. Donald scored a try on début for the Leeds club against the [[Huddersfield Giants]] at [[Headingley Rugby Stadium|Headingley Carnegie Stadium]] in [[Leeds]] on 11 February 2006.

He was the 3rd top try scoring winger in the league in 2006, with only St Helens' [[Ade Gardner]], and Catalans' [[Justin Murphy (rugby league)|Justin Murphy]] scoring more tries than he did.

In the 2007 Super League season, he went on to score a total of 20 tries and has still to miss a match for the Leeds side on completing his third season. In September 2007, Donald was named in the Super League Dream Team. In October 2007, Donald scored a try in Leeds 33–6 victory over St Helens at Old Trafford in the Super League Grand Final.

Donald was named in the [[Super League Dream Team]] for 2008's [[Super League XIII]] season.<ref name="2008 engage Super League Dream Team">{{cite web|url=http://www.superleague.co.uk/article.php?id=11755|title=2008 engage Super League Dream Team|publisher=Super League|date=2008-09-15|accessdate=2008-09-26|url-status=dead|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20100721195910/http://www.superleague.co.uk/article.php?id=11755|archivedate=21 July 2010}}</ref>

Donald played in the [[Super League XIII#Grand Final|2008 Super League Grand Final]] victory over [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]].<ref name="2008 Grand Final">{{cite web|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/7642170.stm|title=2008 Grand Final|publisher=BBC|date=2008-10-04|access-date=2008-10-05}}</ref>

Donald played on the wing for the Leeds club in their 20–28 loss to his former team Manly in the [[2009 World Club Challenge]] at [[Elland Road]] in Leeds.

Donald played in the [[2009 Super League Grand Final]] victory over [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]] at [[Old Trafford]].<ref>{{cite news|last=Fletcher|first=Paul|title=St Helens 10-18 Leeds Rhinos|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/8290503.stm|access-date=22 November 2019|newspaper=BBC Sport|date=10 October 2009}}</ref>

Donald retired from playing at the end of the [[Super League XVI|2010 Super League season]].
